what_it_does: Defines workshop canvas section rendering and an add-divider insertion control. Section wraps a block with selection, move, lock, refine, variation, and remove controls, while AddDivider offers manual block insertion, Vi insertion, and library drag-drop insertion.
observations: Uses global registries and globals: window.WS_BLOCKS, window.WSSection, window.WSAddDivider, and window.WS_resolveLibraryPayload.
Section returns null when section.kind has no matching block definition.
Section delegates block rendering to def.render and updates section.data through onChange.
Toolbar button handlers stop propagation so wrapper selection clicks do not fire from controls.
AddDivider maintains local open and dragOver state via an aliased React useState.
AddDivider accepts library drag payloads with MIME type application/x-cv-library and inserts resolved section payloads.
The block picker menu is styled inline and built from Object.entries(window.WS_BLOCKS).
standouts: Combines editable block chrome with direct block rendering in one wrapper component.
Supports three insertion paths in AddDivider: picker, drag-drop library payload, and Vi-generated options.
Variation selection is visible only for the selected section when variation data exists.
conventions: Uses window namespace assignment for component exposure instead of module exports.
Uses function components and JSX without explicit imports beyond the React useState alias.
Uses compact inline event handlers for toolbar actions.
Uses inline styles for some layout and popup positioning alongside CSS class names.
concerns: AddDivider receives atIdx but does not use it.
tryHandlePayload silently ignores JSON parse or resolver errors with an empty catch block.
Section assumes section, section.data, and callback props are valid without local guards.
The block picker popup positioning is inline and absolute, which may depend on surrounding CSS positioning.
notes: This file is the workshop section chrome plus the insertion divider used between canvas blocks.
questions: Should atIdx determine insertion position, or is insertion position handled by the parent callback closure?
What shape is expected from window.WS_resolveLibraryPayload beyond resolved.kind === "section"?
Should failed drag payload parsing surface any feedback to the user?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches Section's wrapper controls, and the file also includes the AddDivider insertion/drop-target component described by its local comment.
intention_signals: The file appears intended to make each workshop block selectable and controllable while keeping insertion actions available between blocks.
novelty: The divider doubles as a simple add-block affordance, a library drop target, and a Vi option trigger.
contribution: Provides the canvas-level interaction wrapper for individual workshop sections and the between-section insertion affordance, including movement, locking, refinement, variation picking, removal, picker insertion, Vi insertion, and library drop handling.
summary_for_embedding: This file defines React workshop canvas components for wrapping editable sections with toolbar controls and adding blocks through a divider picker, Vi action, or library drag-drop payload.
intention_for_embedding: It was for turning block definitions from a global registry into directly manipulable canvas sections with local insertion controls between them.

symbols.does: Renders one workshop section with toolbar controls and the block's own render output.
symbols.description: Section resolves the block definition from window.WS_BLOCKS using section.kind, applies selected and locked classes, and exposes controls for movement, locking, refinement, variations, and removal. It calls def.render with section.data and wraps data updates through onChange while preserving the rest of the section object.

symbols.does: Renders an between-section add control with picker, drag-drop, and optional Vi insertion.
symbols.description: AddDivider tracks whether its block picker is open and whether a library item is being dragged over it. It displays a divider label, optional Vi action, and a popup grid of available block definitions that call onAdd with the selected block key.

symbols.does: Attempts to parse and insert a dropped library section payload.
symbols.description: tryHandlePayload reads application/x-cv-library data from the drag event, prevents the default drop behavior when payload data exists, and passes the parsed payload through window.WS_resolveLibraryPayload. If the resolved item is a section and onInsertSection is available, it inserts the resolved section and returns true; otherwise it returns false.
